title: Post-Quantum Cryptography and Hybrid Migration · Language Lens: Crypto Go Slhdsa Boundary
summary: A roadmap replaces every algorithm with one new primitive immediately, ignores harvest-now-decrypt-later exposure, and labels a pending module fully compliant. Predict the asset, adversary, security property, trust boundary, and falsifying evidence before opening the governing standard. Planned artifact: Add and verify the slhdsa boundary increment in post-quantum inventory and hybrid migration plan. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging,…
objectives: Explain SLH-DSA standardization, conservative hash-based assumptions, size/performance tradeoffs, and implementation availability limits.
Use Go ML-DSA parameter sets and context-aware signing with explicit key, signature-size, interoperability, and policy tradeoffs.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Cryptography and Go Predict Crypto Go Slhdsa Boundary · Theory Crypto Go Slhdsa Boundary
steps.instruction:
theory case — A theory cryptographic engineering team handles case 5200 during a credential migration window. Build deterministic Go evidence to gate hybrid ML-KEM and ML-DSA migration on inventory, specified combiner, transcript binding, downgrade defense, and rollback; record key custody, terminal failure, rollback, recovery, and residual-risk ownership; then repeat or roll back a nonce, sequence, or key version and reject the stale state. Browser code uses pure state models or the narrow side-effect-free crypto allowlist with fixed fixtures. Production entropy and keys, secret custody, native timing and side channels, networks, TLS, HSM/KMS, certificate systems, FIPS or compliance state, load, faults, restore, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: Explain SLH-DSA standardization, conservative hash-based assumptions, size/performance tradeoffs, and implementation availability limits.
